topic: How toxic sales culture and over-valuation destroyed India's biggest EdTech company
key_belief: When a company optimizes for sales over learning outcomes, it betrays the trust of Indian parents permanently. In India, trust once lost is never recovered. Aggressive EMI-based selling to families who cannot afford it is not growth — it is destruction disguised as scale
outcome: From 22 billion dollar valuation to zero. NCPCR investigation. Thousands of complaints. Mass layoffs. Accounting fraud allegations. Became the single biggest cautionary tale in Indian startup history.
contradicts_western: Blitzscaling justifies aggressive sales. In Indian EdTech, aggressive sales destroys community trust which is your only real moat.
content: Byju's hired thousands of BDAs trained to sell at any cost. Families in Tier 2 cities were pushed into 80,000 rupee EMI packages they could not afford. Refund requests were ignored. Tablets were locked remotely when payments were missed. Students stopped using the product but payments continued. When parents complained publicly on social media the trust collapse was instant and total. Accounting irregularities hid the real burn. Investors lost confidence simultaneously. The lesson — in Indian EdTech, your sales culture IS your brand. You cannot separate them. PW proved the opposite works better.
